text: In this study, we synthesized a unique Cerium (Ce) metal–organic framework (MOF) containing 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid (NTB) as a linker using the hydrothermal method. The obtained Ce-MOF hybrid material possesses the Lewis acid active sites i.e., Ce 3+ ions and triphenylamine cores. Evaluation of catalytic performance towards heterogeneous Fenton-like reaction and catalytic oxidation activity revealed efficient catalytic activity towards oxidation of olefins as well as primary and secondary alcohols along with water pollutants namely organic dyes. Highest catalytic oxidation conversion was observed for cinnamyl alcohol (100%) and styrene (53%) respectively with good selectivity towards styrene oxide (75%) and benzaldehyde (100% under heterogeneous Fenton-like conditions. The as-obtained Ce-MOF hybrid material catalyst was used towards degradation of organic dyes namely rhodamine B (RhB), methyl blue (MeB), Congo red (CR) and direct blue (DB) using hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) as an oxidant. Highest catalytic oxidation performance towards degradation of all the targeted organic water pollutants was achieved. The oxidation performance studies reveals formation of radical hydroxyl ( • OH) species that further opens up the scope for potential application and improvement of catalytic oxidative performance including oxidative degradation of organic pollutants by employing advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) using Ce-MOF hybrid material as an active catalyst. The catalyst is also reused up-to five cycles retaining its catalytic performance without notable decay.
